title: Git-native reasoning graph for persistent code intent capture
context: Claude Code and developers lack persistent access to decision lineage, invariants, and bug patterns. Traditional comments rot; code structure alone doesn't capture intent.
decision: Build a 'Project Brain'—a queryable graph stored in git that extracts and indexes code relationships (symbols, calls, imports) and captures decisions, bug lineage, and invariants. Expose via MCP, CLI, post-commit hooks, and auto-CLAUDE.md generation. Use native tree-sitter + SQLite/FTS5 for structured storage and lookup.
consequences: Developers can record and query structured 'why' for any code location
Claude Code can reason about intent and decision context during interactions
Brain state stays in version control alongside code
Requires maintenance of language-specific extractors (tree-sitter grammars)
Need developer discipline to keep decision records current
Adds complexity and surface area; new subsystem must be kept in sync
alternatives_rejected: Pure documentation (CLAUDE.md) — lacks structured queryability and graph structure
Centralized external service — breaks git-native model, requires network, harder to version-control
WASM tree-sitter — known grammar ABI mismatches with Node 20; native implementation more reliable
Static analysis only — captures syntax but not human intent or decision context
Manual reverse-engineering from git history — doesn't scale, error-prone
